title: Adeno-associated virus with variant capsid and methods of use thereof
abstract: The present disclosure provides recombinant adeno-associated virus (AAV) virions with altered capsid protein, where the recombinant AAV (rAAV) virions exhibit greater infectivity of a cardiac cell compared to wild-type AAV, and where the rAAV virions comprise a heterologous nucleic acid. The present disclosure provides methods of delivering a gene product to a cardiac cell in an individual. The present disclosure provides methods of generating an induced cardiomyocyte-like cell. The present disclosure provides methods of editing the genome of a cardiac cell.
claims: Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A recombinant adeno-associated virus (rAAV) virion comprising: a) a variant AAV capsid protein comprising an amino acid substitution at one or more of E67, S207, N551, and 1698 of the AAV2 capsid amino acid sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: l, or the corresponding position in another parental AAV serotype, wherein the variant AAV capsid protein confers increased infectivity of a cardiac cell, compared to the infectivity of the cardiac cell by an AAV virion comprising a wild- type AAV capsid protein; and b) a heterologous nucleic acid comprising a nucleotide sequence encoding a gene product. 2. The rAAV virion of claim 1 , wherein the variant AAV capsid protein comprises E67A, S207G, N551S, and I698V substitutions. 3. The rAAV virion of claim 1, wherein the cardiac cell is a cardiac fibroblast or a cardiomyocyte. 4. The rAAV virion of claim 1 , wherein the rAAV virion exhibits at least 2-fold increased infectivity of a cardiac cell. 5. The rAAV virion of claim 1, wherein the rAAV virion exhibits at least 5-fold increased infectivity of a cardiac cell. 6. The rAAV virion of any one of claims 1-5, wherein the gene product is a nucleic acid. 7. The rAAV virion of any one of claims 1-5, wherein the gene product is a polypeptide. 8. The rAAV virion of claim 7, wherein the gene product is a reprogramming factor. 9. The rAAV virion of claim 8, wherein the gene product is one or more of Mef2c, Gata4, Myocd, and Tbx5. 10. The rAAV virion of claim 7, wherein the gene product is an angiogenic growth factor. 11. The rAAV virion of claim 10, wherein the growth factor is fibroblast growth factor, platelet-derived growth factor, hepatocyte growth factor, or hypoxia inducible factor. 12. The rAAV virion of claim 7, wherein the polypeptide is sarcoendoplasmic reticulum calcium- ATPase 2a, stromal-derived factor- 1, adenylate cyclase-6, or betaARKct. 13. The rAAV virion of claim 7, wherein the polypeptide is a genome-editing enzyme. 14. The rAAV virion of claim 13, wherein the genome -editing enzyme is a Cas9 polypeptide, a zinc finger nuclease, a TALEN, or an enzymatically inactive type II CRISPR/Cas polypeptide. 15. The rAAV virion of claim 6, wherein the nucleic acid gene product is a guide RNA that binds to genome-editing enzyme. 16. A pharmaceutical composition comprising: a) the rAAV virion of any one of claims 1-15; and b) a pharmaceutically acceptable excipient, diluent, carrier, or buffer. 17. A method of delivering a gene product to a cardiac cell, the method comprising contacting the cardiac cell with the rAAV virion of any one of claims 1-15. 18. A method of delivering a gene product to a cardiac cell in an individual, the method comprising administering to the individual the rAAV virion of any one of claims 1-15. 19. The method of claim 18, wherein said administering is via intramyocardial injection, vascular delivery via the coronary artery, systemic delivery through the superior vena cava, or systemic delivery through a peripheral vein. 20. A method of reprogramming a cardiac fibroblast, the method comprising contacting a cardiac fibroblast with the rAAV virion of claim 8 or claim 9, wherein the reprogramming factor(s) is/are produced in the cardiac fibroblast, and wherein the reprogramming factor(s) induce reprogramming of the cardiac fibroblast to generate a cardiomyocyte-like cell. 21. The method of claim 20, wherein said contacting occurs in vitro, generating an in vitro cardiomyocyte-like cell. 22. The method of claim 21, comprising implanting the cardiomyocyte-like cell into an individual in need thereof. The method of claim 20, wherein said contacting occurs in vivo.
